skill.name: skill-template
skill.description: Reusable template for creating structured DocEngineering skills with validation steps.
skill.summary: Create structured DocEngineering skills with repeatable workflow and validation guidance.
skill.author: 64andrewwalker

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and shell execution patterns, but those specific matches are false positives in documentation text and an argparse description. The skill does declare Bash(python:*) permission and includes a simple Python entrypoint, so the remaining risk is low and tied to bounded script execution.

low_findings.title: Bounded Python Command Permission
low_findings.description: The skill declares Bash(python:*) in allowed tools. This permits Python command execution, but reviewed instructions direct use of deterministic local scripts and the included script only parses arguments and prints status messages.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The executable permission is explicit and the reviewed script has no network, filesystem write, crypto, or shell subprocess behavior. Risk remains low because Python execution can be expanded if future template users add unsafe scripts.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Documentation Syntax
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code references, not executable Ruby or shell code. The reported weak cryptography locations contain descriptive text only and no cryptographic algorithm usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited SKILL.md lines are prose or Markdown code formatting, and scripts/run.py line 9 is an argparse description string. No evidence found of weak cryptography or backtick command execution.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable skill structures that are easy to review and validate. This template gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear workflow for building document engineering skills.
actual_capabilities: Defines a reusable folder pattern for new DocEngineering skills.
Specifies a scout, analyze, execute, and verify workflow.
Provides a Python entrypoint with a basic self-test mode.
Documents error handling for missing inputs and hard gates.
Encourages strict output formats through templates or schemas.
limitations: It is a starting template, not a complete domain-specific skill.
It does not include production validation schemas or assets.
It only provides a minimal Python script for self-testing.
Users must add concrete instructions before using it for real workflows.

use_cases.title: Create a New Documentation Skill
use_cases.description: Use the template as a starting structure for a focused document automation skill.

use_cases.title: Standardize Skill Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the workflow sections to make new skills easier to inspect and approve.

use_cases.title: Prototype Validation Gates
use_cases.description: Extend the self-test script into checks that confirm inputs, outputs, and hard gates.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template to outline a new documentation skill for my workflow. Ask for missing inputs before writing files.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the scout, analyze, execute, and verify steps for a skill that processes technical documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Design validation gates for this skill template, including required inputs, expected outputs, and failure conditions.

prompt_templates.title: Review for Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this completed skill against the template. Identify missing instructions, unsafe tool usage, weak validation, and release blockers.

output_examples.input: Create a documentation skill from this template.
output_examples.output: A structured skill plan with required inputs, workflow steps, verification checks, and explicit error handling.
A short pass status confirming the template script can execute in self-test mode.

output_examples.input: Review a draft skill for release readiness.
output_examples.output: A concise review listing missing gates, unclear instructions, and publication blockers.

best_practices: Replace placeholder instructions with domain-specific, testable steps.
Keep validation scripts deterministic and limited to required files.
Define output formats before adding automation logic.
anti_patterns: Do not publish the template without adding a real workflow.
Do not grant broad shell access when a narrow script permission is enough.
Do not rely on prose checks when a schema or test can verify output.

faq.question: Is this a finished documentation skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a template for creating production-oriented DocEngineering skills.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Does it execute external commands?
faq.answer: It permits Python Bash commands and includes a minimal local Python script.

faq.question: Does the included script change files?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The script only parses arguments and prints status messages.

faq.question: What should users add first?
faq.answer: Users should add concrete inputs, domain instructions, validation checks, and output formats.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low?
faq.answer: The static findings were false positives, but bounded Python execution still requires normal review.
